rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Defendants Amended Answer To The Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS AS AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO DEFENDANTS AMENDED ANSWER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is DENIED. Motion set for hearing within 30 days of the current trial date (which is the 3rd trial date in this action). The title of the motion, the points and authorities and the Notice of the motion do not align: the title of the motion indicates that Plaintiff seeks judgment on the pleadings as to the "amended answer," a pleadings that this not on file and is not properly before the Court; the notice of motion purports to seek statutory judgment on the pleadings as to Answer; the points and authorities include arguments pertaining to both, the answer and amended answer. Moving party provides no legal basis for the Court to consider an unfiled amended answer (attached directly to the points and authorities without any attempt to properly introduce it) on a motion for judgment on the pleadings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HV-645 HARRISON, INC., VS. SONY INTERACTIVE ENTERTAINMENT NETWORK AMERICA LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT SONY INTERACTIVE ENTERTAINMENT LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS SONY INTERACTIVE NETWORK AMERICA LLC ) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding waiver of timely billing provisions of the lease. See additional facts 34, 35 and 36. Defendants also dispute Plaintiff's additional fact 26 and present evidence in reply to support this disputed fact. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. CCP 437c(f)(1). None of the purported issues for summary adjudication "completely disposed of a cause of action, and affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." Motion for Summary Adjudication is framed by the pleadings which assert that "Sony has failed to pay Additional Charges incurred for the years 2018 through 2021 the past due amount owed to Plaintiff is $575,355.15, plus accrued interest." (Complaint, paragraph 8). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE T. LUI ET AL VS. GARY LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2024 line 8. DEPLAINTIFF LAWRENCE LUI , AS CO-TRUSTEES OF THE LUI FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 27, 2005, TERRY LUI, GLORIA LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY Property is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority to support Plaintiff's asserting that defendants were unlawfully detaining the property at the time of the filing of the complaint, which served as a notice of termination of license, i.e. before Defendants were notified of the termination. Motion for Summary Judgment is framed by the pleadings. Additionally, Plaintiffs fail to provide admissible competent evidence that defendants remain in possession. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
